pi_items.source: setup_task.sh:41 - INSERT statement explicitly creates student with first_name='Philip'
setup_task.sh:41 - INSERT statement explicitly creates student with last_name='Williams'
setup_task.sh:60 - INSERT statement explicitly creates course with title='Introduction to Psychology'
setup_task.sh:60 - INSERT statement explicitly creates course with short_name='PSY101' (used as course code in OpenSIS)
setup_task.sh:72 - INSERT statement creates course_period with short_name='PSY101-1', which represents Section 1
task.json description and verifier.py logic confirm the target is to remove the schedule record linking Philip Williams to PSY101 (lines 54-63 in verifier.py)

privileged_info_summary: This task operates on a local OpenSIS (open-source Student Information System v9.2) installation running on MariaDB. All student and course data is synthetic, created via SQL INSERT statements during task setup. The student Philip Williams (middle name J, male, DOB 2005-09-15, Grade 10) is created in the synthetic opensis database during setup_task.sh and does not represent a real person. The course Introduction to Psychology (course code PSY101, Social Sciences, Grade 10, 1.0 credits, Section 1) is synthetic and created for this benchmark task. All metadata values match exactly with the verified data creation statements in setup_task.sh. The verifier checks that: (1) the schedule record linking Philip to PSY101 is removed (40 pts), (2) the student record is preserved (15 pts), (3) the course record is preserved (15 pts), (4) schedule count decreased (15 pts), and (5) visual verification of schedule interaction (15 pts).
